expect: Wiring citation_amplify.py sink surfacing into orient_monitors.py + open_lane.py will make high-Sharpe sinks visible at two decision points (orient and lane creation). Baseline re-citation rate is 0%. Target: >5% within 30 sessions. Prediction: sink visibility alone is necessary but insufficient — agents need citation prompts at the moment they write Cites: headers.
actual: Two mechanisms implemented: (1) orient_monitors.py section_trace_amplification upgraded to show both hub gaps (forgotten hubs) and high-Sharpe sinks (zero incoming citations). (2) open_lane.py now suggests domain-specific sinks at lane creation time. Baseline: 283 sinks (22.5%), 15 high-Sharpe sinks (S>=7), L-601 dominates at 478 in-degree (7x gap to #2).
diff: Expected: visibility alone insufficient. Cannot confirm yet — need 30 sessions to measure re-citation rate. The mechanism is structural (creation-time suggestion per L-601) not voluntary.

baseline.measurement_note: Re-citation rate = fraction of sink lessons that gain >=1 incoming citation in the next 30 sessions

mechanisms_implemented: orient_monitors.py: section_trace_amplification now surfaces high-Sharpe sinks alongside hub gaps
open_lane.py: domain-specific sink suggestions at lane creation (F-STIG1 creation-time enforcement)
follow_up: Re-measure sink_count and re_citation_rate at S560 (30 sessions)
If re-citation rate < 5%, add third mechanism: Cites: header auto-suggestion in lesson template
